title: Fix stale requires-python (~=3.8) in pyproject.toml; charm targets Ubuntu 24.04 / Python 3.12
summary: pyproject.toml declares requires-python = "~=3.8" (line 6), but charmcraft.yaml builds on ubuntu@24.04 (Python 3.12) and the file is copyright 2025. Python 3.8 is EOL (Oct 2024). The stale, malformed specifier also makes uv emit a warning on every tox run.
why_good_first_issue: Single-line change in one file; clear correct value (3.10+/3.12); no architecture knowledge needed; the wrong-vs-right is self-evident and tooling already flags it.
repro_steps: 1) grep -n requires-python pyproject.toml -> line 6: requires-python = "~=3.8". 2) grep platforms in charmcraft.yaml -> ubuntu@24.04:amd64 (Python 3.12). 3) Run tox -e unit and observe uv warning.
repro_result: Observed: pyproject.toml:6 requires-python = "~=3.8". charmcraft.yaml platforms = ubuntu@24.04:amd64 (ships Python 3.12). tox -e unit emits: "warning: The requires-python specifier (~=3.8) in prometheus-pushgateway-k8s uses the tilde specifier without a patch version. This will be interpreted as >=3.8, <4." Expected: requires-python aligned with build base (e.g. >=3.10 or ~=3.12.0).
fix_hint: Change pyproject.toml line 6 to a valid, current floor matching the 24.04 base, e.g. requires-python = ">=3.10" (or ~=3.12.0). Re-run tox -e unit to confirm the uv warning disappears.
notes: Unit suite passes (27 passed) on the VM; this is purely a metadata correctness/cleanup fix. ops resolved = 2.21.1.

title: Use modern ops main entrypoint: replace from ops.main import main with from ops import main
summary: src/charm.py imports the deprecated entrypoint via from ops.main import main (line 24) and calls main(PrometheusPushgatewayK8SOperatorCharm) (line 355). ops>=2 recommends from ops import main; the ops.main.main symbol is the legacy form.
why_good_first_issue: Two-line, mechanical change in a single file; recommended modern API is unambiguous and matches house style across other COS charms; unit tests cover the charm so a regression would surface.
repro_steps: 1) grep -n 'ops.main' and 'main(' in src/charm.py -> line 24 from ops.main import main; line 355 main(PrometheusPushgatewayK8SOperatorCharm). 2) Compare against ops>=2 docs recommending from ops import main.
repro_result: Observed: src/charm.py:24 'from ops.main import main' and src/charm.py:355 'main(PrometheusPushgatewayK8SOperatorCharm)'. Expected (ops>=2, pinned ops 2.21.1): 'from ops import main'. The dependency floor is ops[tracing]>=2.2 (pyproject.toml line 9), so the modern form is available.
fix_hint: Replace line 24 with 'from ops import main'; the call on line 355 stays the same. Run tox -e unit and tox -e static to confirm.
notes: Lower priority than the requires-python fix; both are tiny cleanups. Unit tests pass (27 passed).
